# FOSBAAS <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/R-package-blue" align="right" alt="R package"/>

**F**undamentals **o**f **S**ports **B**usiness **A**nalytics **a**nd **S**trategy

`FOSBAAS` is the companion R package for the book *Fundamentals of Sports
Business Analytics and Strategy*. It bundles the example data sets used
throughout the book together with the simulation and helper functions that
generated them, so readers can reproduce every analysis end to end.

The data describe a fictional professional sports franchise — its tickets,
customers, demographics, promotions, secondary-market sales and ballpark
operations. The functions are intentionally simple and meant for teaching, not
production: they have minimal error checking and each illustrates one idea from
the book.

## Installation

```r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("Justin-Watkins/FOSBAAS")
```

## Data sets

Because `LazyData` is enabled, each data set is available by name once the
package is attached.

| Data set | Rows × cols | Used in | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aggregated_crm_data` | 5,000 × 3 | Ch 4 | Rep-level CRM calls and revenue |
| `customer_data` | 200,000 × 2 | Ch 5, 7 | Customer IDs and names |
| `demographic_data` | 200,000 × 14 | Ch 5, 7 | Purchased demographic attributes |
| `fa_survey_data` | 10,000 × 26 | Ch 5 | Survey ratings for factor analysis |
| `manifest_data` | 44,705 × 8 | Ch 7 | Seat manifest and pricing |
| `perceptual_data` | 3 × 10 | Ch 9 | Brand-attribute counts (perceptual map) |
| `scan_data` | 900 × 4 | Ch 10 | Per-minute gate scans |
| `season_data` | 243 × 12 | Ch 3, 6, 8 | Three seasons of games and sales |
| `secondary_data` | 853,444 × 9 | Ch 6 | Secondary-market ticket sales |
| `customer_renewals` | 13,706 × 10 | Ch 1, 7 | Season-ticket renewal panel |
| `wait_times_data` | 900 × 5 | Ch 10 | Concession wait times |
| `wait_times_distribution_data` | 3,000 × 5 | Ch 10 | Larger wait-time sample |
| `freq_table_data` | 161 × 3 | Ch 10 | Empirical scan-count distribution |
| `freq_table_data_bin` | 23 × 3 | Ch 10 | Binned service-time distribution |

## Functions

The simulation helpers are grouped into families (see `?FOSBAAS`):

- **Season simulation** — `f_build_season()` and helpers (`f_create_dates()`,
  `f_select_team_for_series()`, `f_assign_promotion()`, `f_attend_coefficient()`, …)
- **Customer & demographics** — `f_build_demographic_data()` and helpers
  (`f_get_age()`, `f_get_distance()`, `f_demographics_*()`, …)
- **Lead scoring** — `f_create_lead_scoring_data()` and helpers
  (`f_calculate_tenure()`, `f_calculate_spend()`, `f_renewal_assignment()`, …)
- **Operations & queueing** — `f_get_MMKN()`, `f_get_wait_times()`,
  `f_get_scan_data()`, `f_get_line_length()`
- **Utilities** — `f_add_zero()`, `f_define_line()`, `f_build_freq_table()`

## Examples

```r
library(FOSBAAS)

# A bundled data set
head(season_data)

# Simulate a season of home games
season <- f_build_season(seed1 = 3000, season_year = 2024, seed2 = 714,
                         num_games = 81, seed3 = 366, num_bbh = 5,
                         num_con = 3, num_oth = 5, seed4 = 309, seed5 = 25,
                         mean_sales = 29000, sd_sales = 3500)

# Solve an M/M/k/N queue (2 servers, capacity 5)
f_get_MMKN(k = 2, N = 5, ta = 10, ts = 8)
```

## Reproducibility note

This release fixes several long-standing bugs in the simulation functions
(seeding, dead branches, a global-variable reference and others). Because some
of those fixes change the random draws, re-running the simulation functions now
produces different output than earlier versions. The bundled `.rda` data sets
are unchanged; if you re-knit analyses that call the simulation functions
directly, expect the generated numbers to differ.
